_meta.purpose: Two-level dance taxonomy for the practice surface. Level 1 = family (picker sections), level 2 = style. Users can always log a family without a style, or free-text a style not listed.
_meta.taxonomyVerification: Top-level split verified against common genre classifications (ballet / modern-contemporary / hip-hop-street / latin-ballroom-social / folk): the proposed six families are defensible with two adjustments made: (1) ballroom absorbs the competitive 'International Latin' five (cha-cha, rumba, samba, paso doble, jive) plus swing — dancesport and studio convention — while the 'latin' family means the social/street-latin scene (salsa, bachata, etc.), matching how classes and socials are actually marketed; (2) ballet-contemporary is broadened to the studio/concert grouping and also carries jazz, lyrical, and tap. Sources: danceus.org/latin-dance, uk.harlequinfloors.com popular-types-of-dance, dancehouseproductions.com/glossary/dance-styles.

healthkit.mapping: contextTags drive the HK workout type: social -> HKWorkoutActivityType.socialDance (Apple: 'dancing with a partner or partners, such as swing, salsa, or folk dances'); class/practice/rehearsal/performance -> HKWorkoutActivityType.cardioDance. Both types exist, iOS 14+.
healthkit.barreNote: HKWorkoutActivityType.barre exists as its own type; if style is fitness/barre the app SHOULD map to .barre regardless of contextTag.

notes: kizomba is Angolan (not Latin American) by origin but is danced and taught inside the salsa/bachata social scene, so it lives under latin by scene convention — descriptor says so honestly.
'cumbria' in the source spec was a typo for cumbia (Colombian).

Swing (east/west coast, lindy) placed under ballroom: East Coast Swing is an American Rhythm ballroom dance and Apple's own socialDance examples name swing; a standalone 'swing' family was judged too thin for level 1.

styles.label: Kizomba
styles.descriptor: Angolan origin; danced in the Latin social scene
